shared_properties: Hazard art keeps a crisp dark outline and metallic interior clusters for dungeon readability.
Frame size stays 24x24px to match the GDD hazard footprint.
The blade silhouette remains centered so rotation feels stable at 24 FPS.
differences: Sprite sheet is 192x24px with eight rotation frames in one row.
Tooth highlights alternate around the rim so clockwise motion reads without motion blur.
Cold steel tones dominate the body while the hub stays darker to preserve depth.

context.usage: Fixed-position spinning saw used in late-stage damage gauntlets.

shape.silhouette: Circular blade with eight angled teeth and a dark center hub.
shape.collision_read: Outer tooth ring stays evenly spaced for predictable hazard read.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet size is exactly 192x24px with eight 24x24px frames.
Each tooth remains readable at 1x scale throughout the 24 FPS spin loop.
The rotation loop cycles seamlessly from frame eight back to frame one without a visible hitch.

reference_images.label: slip_down_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds stone texture breakup, low-key lighting, and vertical shaft composition for the active environment asset.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteSheetBundle with a TextureAtlasLayout and high-frequency animation timer.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load through AssetServer during the Loading state and attach the atlas to saw hazard prefabs.
runtime_notes: Use Bevy 0.13 with ImagePlugin::default_nearest() so every asset stays pixel-perfect at 4x upscale.
Drive gameplay motion in FixedUpdate at 60 FPS and keep art timing on deterministic timers.
Keep the rotation purely visual if collision remains a simple circle in gameplay code.
